verdict_reasoning: SRC (p60-Src) is canonically a non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and a polybasic amino acid cluster. Under baseline conditions, the kinase domain faces the cytoplasm; it is not accessible from the extracellular face. However, a 2026 Science paper (autophagolysosomal exocytosis, ALE) provides direct gene-specific experimental evidence that Src is noncanonically translocated and inverted onto the outer cell surface in cancer, both in vitro and in vivo — confirmed in cancer cell lines, xenografts, and primary patient tumor samples. Antibody-based therapies targeting this extracellular Src (eSrc) mediated anti-tumor activity. This constitutes documented cell-state-induced (oncogenic transformation + dysregulated autophagic flux) outer-leaflet display, qualifying for contextual / cell_state_induced. Pre-no checklist: no germline/tissue-restricted display; not a lysosomal TM protein; no stable TM-partner tethering; no constitutive dual-localization PM pool; but oncogenic cell-state outer-leaflet display IS documented for this protein specifically.
